entities.name: Airports of Thailand Public Company Limited

financial.note: AOT utilizes state-linked credit facilities for major infrastructure expansion; interest rate hikes increase the cost of servicing long-term debt.
Provides significant financing for terminal upgrades and runway construction under the Suvarnabhumi Phase 2 & 3 projects.

supply_chain.note: Duty-free and retail concessions account for over 40% of non-aeronautical revenue; performance is directly tied to passenger traffic and spend-per-head.
Exclusive fuel hydrant provider at major airports; flight volume directly correlates with AOT's landing fees and BAFS service volumes.
Primary contractor for infrastructure; rising construction material costs or project delays negatively impact AOT's CAPEX efficiency.

competitors.note: Regional hub competition for international transit traffic between Europe/US and Asia-Pacific.
Bangkok Airways operates private airports (Samui, Trat) which compete for high-yield luxury tourist segments.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The single largest driver of international Passenger Service Charges (PSC) and retail concession revenue.
A weaker THB incentivizes inbound tourism, increasing airport throughput, though it marginally affects foreign-denominated debt valuation.
Indirect impact; high fuel costs lead to flight cancellations and reduced frequency by airline clients, lowering landing/parking fee revenue.
